volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Stichus (Section 1.3.234_235 - Section 2.2.330b)

sections.label: Section 1.3.234_235
sections.translation: An auction of no great value , by my troth . Hunger has taken hold of the very deepest recess of the fellow ’ s stomach . I ’ ll accost the man .

sections.label: Section 1.3.237b
sections.original: Quis haec est quae advorsum it mihi ?
sections.translation: Who ’ s this that ’ s coming towards me ? Why , surely this is Crocotium , the maid-servant of Epignomus .

sections.label: Section 1.3.239
sections.translation: My respects , Gelasimus .

sections.label: Section 1.3.239b
sections.translation: That ’ s not my name .

sections.label: Section 1.3.240
sections.original: Certo mecastor id fuit nomen tibi .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , for sure that used to be your name .

sections.label: Section 1.3.241
sections.original: Fuit disertim , verum id usu perdidi :
sections.translation: Distinctly it was so , but I ’ ve lost it by use . Now I ’ m called Miccotrogus [*] from what is fact .

sections.label: Section 1.3.243
sections.original: † Eu ecastor , risi te hodie multum .
sections.translation: O dear ! I ’ ve laughed a good deal at you to-day .

sections.label: Section 1.3.243b
sections.translation: When ? or in what place ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.244_245
sections.translation: Here , when you were carrying on a most worthless auction .

sections.label: Section 1.3.244_245b
sections.translation: How now ; did you really hear it ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.246b
sections.translation: Aye , and one really right worthy of yourself .

sections.label: Section 1.3.247
sections.translation: Where are you bound for now ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.247b
sections.translation: For yourself .

sections.label: Section 1.3.247c
sections.translation: Why have you come ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.247d
sections.translation: Philumena bade me ask you by all means to come to visit her at her house this instant , together with me .

sections.label: Section 1.3.250
sections.original: Ego illo mehercle vero eo quantum potest .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , but I ’ ll surely come there as fast as I can . Are the entrails cooked [*] by this ? With how many lambs has she been sacrificing ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.252
sections.translation: Indeed , she hasn ’ t been sacrificing at all .

sections.label: Section 1.3.252b
sections.translation: How ? What does she want with me , then ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.253b
sections.translation: I think that she ’ s going to ask you for ten measures of wheat .

sections.label: Section 1.3.254b
sections.translation: Or me rather ask it of her ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.255
sections.original: Immo ut a vobis mutuom nobis dares .
sections.translation: No ; that you yourself should lend them to us .

sections.label: Section 1.3.256
sections.original: Nega esse quod dem nec mihi nec mutuom ,
sections.translation: Tell her that I ’ ve nothing to give myself , or that she could wish to borrow , nor anything whatever , except this cloak that I have on . Even my very tongue that so freely used to offer itself [*] I ’ ve sold as well .

sections.label: Section 1.3.258b
sections.translation: How ? Have you got no tongue ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.259_260b
sections.translation: Why , the former one , that used to say here , take me [*] , I ’ ve lost : see , here ’ s one now that says give me .

sections.label: Section 1.3.262
sections.translation: A curse may the Gods give you ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.3.262b
sections.translation: Aye , if a curse you want , this same tongue will give you that .

sections.label: Section 1.3.263
sections.original: Quid nunc ? ituru'siturus es an non ?
sections.translation: Well now , are you coming or not ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.263b
sections.translation: Well , be off home ;

sections.label: Section 1.3.264_265
sections.original: iam illo venturum dicito . propera atque abi .—
sections.translation: tell her I ’ ll be there this moment ; make haste and be off . ( CROCOTIUM goes into the house .)

sections.label: Section 1.3.266
sections.original: demiror quid illaec me ad se arcessi iusserit ,
sections.translation: I wonder why she has requested me to be fetched to her , who has never , before this day , requested that I should be fetched to her , ever since her husband left . I wonder what it can be ; except it is for some experiment to be made upon me ; I ’ ll go see what she wants .

sections.label: Section 1.3.270
sections.original: sed eccum Pinacium eius puerum . hoc vide ,
sections.translation: But see , here ’ s her boy , Pinacium . Look at that now ; how very facetiously and just like a picture [*] does he stand ? Full many a time , for sure , in good troth , has he poured out for me the wine , almost unmixed , right cleverly into a very tiny cup [*] indeed .

sections.label: Section 2.1.274
sections.original: Mercúrius , Iovis qui núntius perhibétur , numquam aequé patri
sections.translation: Mercury , who is said to be the messenger of Jove , never bore such pleasing tidings to his father , as I shall e ’ en now be telling to my mistress .

sections.label: Section 2.1.276
sections.original: ítaque onustum péctus porto laétitia lubéntiaque
sections.translation: So loaded do I bear my breast with joy and with delight ; and really I don ’ t care to speak a syllable but in a highflown style . The charms of all the loves and graces do I bring ; my heart , too , is overleaping its banks , and overflowing with joyousness .

sections.label: Section 2.1.280
sections.original: própera , Pinacium , pedes hortare , honesta dicta factis
sections.translation: Now have you the means of acquiring glory , fame , and honor ; make haste , Pinacium , exhort your feet to swiftness , grace your message by your deeds , and come to the rescue of your mistress in her need ⋯ who is so wretched in awaiting the arrival of her husband , Epignomus ; just as becomes her does she dote upon her husband , and anxiously long for him . Now , Pinacium ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.285
sections.original: age út placet , curre ut lubet , cave quémquam flocci feceris ,
sections.translation: do as pleases you , run on , just as you like ; take care and regard no person at the value of a straw ; thrust them from the path with your elbows ; make right smooth your way . If a , king shall come in your way , upset the king himself forthwith .

sections.label: Section 2.1.288
sections.translation: Why , I wonder [*] , is Pinacium running so overladen with baggage [*] ? He ’ s carrying a rod , and a basket , and a fish-hook .

sections.label: Section 2.1.290
sections.original: Sed tandem , opinor , aequiust eram mihi esse supplicem
sections.translation: But yet , I think ’ tis proper that my mistress should come with entreaty to me , and that she should send envoys to me , and gifts of gold , and chariots in which for me to be borne , for I can ’ t go on foot . Therefore I shall now go back . ( Turns back .) I think it is only proper that I should be approached and addressed with entreaties . And do you really think that it ’ s mere nonsense or nothing at all that I am now acquainted with ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.295
sections.original: tantum a portu adporto bonum , tam gaudium grande adfero ,
sections.translation: Blessings so great am I carrying from the harbour , joys so extensive am I bringing , that hardly could my mistress herself presume to wish this of the Gods , if she were to know it . And am I to carry it , then , of my own accord ? It pleases me not , nor do I think that the duty of a man . This way does it seem to be better suited to this news of mine ; let her come to meet me , let her entreat me to communicate to her this news .

sections.label: Section 2.1.300
sections.translation: Haughtiness and pride befit prosperous fortunes . But , at last , when I reconsider it , how could she know that I know this ? ( Turning round .) Well , I can ’ t do otherwise than return , than speak , than relate it at length , and relieve my mistress of her grief , and both mightily increase the good deeds of my ancestors , and present her with a comfort unhoped for and opportune .

sections.label: Section 2.1.305
sections.original: contundam facta Talthubi contemnamque omnis nuntios ;
sections.translation: I ’ ll outdo the deeds of Talthybius [*] , and I ’ ll set all messengers at nought , and at the same time I ’ ll think about the running at the Olympic games . But this distance [*] is far too short for the course ; how sorry for it I am . How ’ s this ? I see the door ’ s closed . I ’ ll go and knock at the door . ( Knocks at the door of PHILUMENA ’ S house .) Open , and make haste , cause the door to be thrown open ; away with all delay .

sections.label: Section 2.1.310
sections.original: nimis haéc res sine curá geritur . vide quám dudum hic asto ét pulto .
sections.translation: This matter is attended to too carelessly ; see how long I ’ ve been standing here and knocking . Are you indulging yourselves with a nap ? I ’ ll try whether the door or my arms and feet are the stronger . ( Knocks and kicks .) I wish much that this door would run away from its master , that for that reason it might meet with a heavy punishment [*] . I ’ m tired of knocking . Well , be this the last for you .

sections.label: Section 2.1.315
sections.translation: I ’ ll go and accost him . ( Accosts PINACIUM .) Good day to you .

sections.label: Section 2.1.316b
sections.translation: And good day to you .

sections.label: Section 2.1.317
sections.translation: Are you turned fisherman , then ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.318
sections.translation: How long is it since you ate ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.319_320
sections.original: Vnde ís ? quid fers ? quid féstinas ?
sections.translation: Whence come you ? What are you carrying ? Why are you in a hurry ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.319_320b
sections.original: Tua quód nil refert , né cures .
sections.translation: About that which is no business of yours , don ’ t you trouble yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.1.321
sections.translation: What ’ s there in that ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.321b
sections.translation: Snakes , for you to eat .

sections.label: Section 2.1.321c
sections.translation: Why are you so pettish ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.321d
sections.translation: If you had any shame , you wouldn ’ t address me .

sections.label: Section 2.1.323
sections.translation: May I learn the truth from you ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.324_325
sections.translation: You may ; this day you ’ ll get no dinner .

sections.label: Section 2.2.326
sections.original: Quisnam , ópsecro , has frangit fóres ? ubi est ?
sections.translation: Who now , pray , is breaking this door down ? ( To GELASIMUS .) Are you doing this ? Do you come to me like an enemy ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.327
sections.translation: My respects to you ; I come at your bidding .

sections.label: Section 2.2.327b
sections.translation: And is it for that reason you are breaking down my door ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.328
sections.original: Tuos ínclama , tui délinquont : ego quíd me velles vísebam ;
sections.translation: Scold your own people ; the offenders are your own . I came to see what you wanted me for . Why , for my own part , I pitied this door .

sections.label: Section 2.2.329b
sections.translation: For that reason your assistance was given so very readily .

sections.label: Section 2.2.330
sections.original: Quisnam híc loquitur tam própe nos ?
sections.translation: Pray , who ’ s that , talking here so near to us ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.330b
sections.translation: Pinacium .
